title: Race-Lap Query Ranker As Auxiliary Expert
summary: A stop call is partly a relative decision: among all drivers on this lap, who is most pit-ready now?
rationale: A stop call is partly a relative decision: among all drivers on this lap, who is most pit-ready now? Ranking objectives are designed for within-group ordering and are a natural fit for grouped race-lap competition among drivers.
implementation_hint: Train a CatBoost or LightGBM ranking model with `group_id = Year|Race|LapNumber`, target = `PitNextLap`, and evaluate via OOF predictions stacked into the main AUC model. Start with QuerySoftMax/LambdaMart or optimize classification while tracking QueryAUC. Compare against pure classification on the same features.
expected_effect: Often improves the ordering of borderline cases within each lap even if absolute calibration is worse; stacking can convert that better ordering into AUC gain.
risk: Groups with very few positives are noisy, and ranking scores are not calibrated probabilities by themselves. Use only as an auxiliary OOF feature or blend component.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
